qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Purchased downloadable URLs are fetched server-side without private-address validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Distinct root cause and code path: this finding is an admin-seeded purchased-download SSRF in `DownloadableProductController::download()`, not server-side template evaluation of customer order or address fields.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Theme static content strips only script tags and renders surviving HTML event handlers raw
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is stored HTML/event-handler injection in theme static content rendered on the storefront home page; it does not involve checkout or customer-address data being evaluated as server-side templates.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's checkout or address SSTI root cause. The advisory is about customer-controlled order/address fields being evaluated as templates in admin or customer views, while the session's qualifying finals are a purchased-download SSRF and a theme static-content stored XSS.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes `{{7*7}}` injected by a normal customer during checkout or address creation becoming `49` in `/admin/sales/orders` and customer address views, which is server-side template evaluation of stored customer-controlled fields.

`downloadable_purchased_link_ssrf` instead centers on `DownloadableProductController::download()` calling `copy()` on an admin-supplied downloadable URL and relaying loopback or internal HTTP responses.

`theme_static_content_stored_xss` instead centers on `ThemeCustomizationRepository::update()` only stripping `<script>` tags and `shop::home.index` rendering surviving HTML event handlers from theme static content.

`artifacts/component_family_checklist.md` explicitly states that SSTI-specific render paths were inspected and pruned, while the surviving accepted render-family issue was stored XSS rather than server-side template injection.
